note: AUC course labels often use dual numbering like SUBJECT old/new. The seed stores the current code on the right side of the slash as the canonical course code.
The catalog program page lists General Electives as 3-15 credits without enumerating eligible courses. The seed sets requiredCredits to 9 by balancing the published 132-credit degree total.

If a course appears in both the university core page and a program-specific requirement section, the course row is classified by the program-specific section while graduation requirement eligibility keeps the source overlap.
Variable-credit course listed as 1-2 credit hours; seed uses the maximum value because the schema requires one integer.
Variable-credit course listed as 1-3 credit hours; seed uses the maximum value because the schema requires one integer.
Variable-credit course listed as 1-3 credit hours; seed uses the maximum value because the schema requires one integer.
These catalog pages do not provide a description section, so the seed leaves description as an empty string per the normalization schema.
These catalog pages do not specify concrete offering terms, so semesterAvailability defaults to ['Fall', 'Spring'] per the normalization schema.
These catalog pages explicitly mention fall, winter, spring and summer offerings. Because the schema has no Winter term, semesterAvailability is normalized to ['All'].
